title: About me
desc: I'm Mike. I'm interested in applying deep learning to cross-disciplinary problems in astrophysics, earth observation, medical imaging, and other fields. My CV is here.

title: The Platonic Universe
desc: Do foundation models see the same sky 🧐? We point models trained on natural imagery at galaxies from major sky surveys -- and find they converge on the same representations, despite never seeing space before. The bigger the model, the more they agree.

title: The Multimodal Universe
desc: A huge 100TB dataset of crossmatched multimodal astronomical data, taken from astronomy's most important telescopes and observatories. Packaged up just right for all your deep learning needs <3 .

title: AstroPT
desc: Can we apply large observation models to astronomy? Of course we can! We find that neural scaling laws can predict how well a neural net performs on astronomically useful downstream tasks. We also suggest a FOSS approach for further developing these models.

title: EarthPT
desc: A Large Observation Model for Earth Observation that can accurately forecast the future. We can also extract the latent space of the model to find the most important features for downstream tasks.

title: Astronomia ex machina
desc: A review on neural networks in astronomy, where we predict that enormous foundation models will play a big role in astronomy's future. The mortarboard icon points to my thesis, which this review is based off of.

title: AstroDDPM
desc: Diffusion models can dream up galaxies that are indistinguishable from the real thing -- physically and visually. They can also zap satellite trails and 'galaxify' any image. Click here to cycle through some fake galaxies.

title: Pix2Prof
desc: What if we captioned galaxies like images? An encoder-decoder model turns galaxy photos into light profile data -- basically teaching a neural net to describe what it sees in the language of astrophysics.

title: SAGAN
desc: A GAN that generates fake astronomical observations so good they fool statisticians. We pushed it to its limits and generated a 7.8 billion pixel synthetic observation of deep space.

title: Neuralnet-MCG
desc: CNNs that diagnose heart attacks from ECG scans and 3D magnetocardiogram video. The model correctly learns that the ST segment is the smoking gun for myocardial infarctions -- no cardiologist needed to tell it that.

title: Heart-CNN
desc: A 3D siamese CNN that spots dead cells in heart tomography scans. Point it at a heart, it finds the damage.
